desc:
This path, being only a minor trail of the many roads stretched out between
Mithlond and the Dwarven Homes, does not see much use. It follows a natural vein
of open space through the forest, sometimes not more than a couple of pole
lengths in width, but most often it measures up to a hundred feet across. While
the path leads the way north and east, a gap in the deep, dark forest leers
wickedly at you from the west.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
You are moving on a path through the outer limits of a forest. Neither the
forest to your east or north are as dense as the imposing forest surrounding the
path going west, and south you clearly see the forest breaking up into open
grassland.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
You stand on the borderland between forest and fields. To the north the forest
closes in on the beaten path, only letting out sounds of wildlife from its dark
recesses. A southward gaze shows you the path making a zigzagging pattern
through a rustic scene of fields and foothills.
name: Trail beside a Forest

desc:
This flimsy path lies along the edge of a forest. The fir trees and bushes form
a thick wall to the west and south, seemingly as dense as the wild and mighty
forests of Far Harad. The path continues north alongside the forest's edge, and
east towards rich green fields.
name: Trail beside a Forest

desc:
The trail is well-used and easy to travel on. All around you the green grass
bends softly in the wind and the chatter of many small birds can be heard. To
the north and west a great forest begins and to the south and east vast plains
and green hills dominate the landscape.
name: Trail near a Forest

desc:
This trail seems to be frequently used, it is even and smooth, which makes
travelling easy. It continues north and east, and further east you see some
foothills, which rolls on for a long distance. To the west you notice a forested
area, which seems to have rich animal life, just like the woods that lies
southeast from here.
name: A Winding Trail

desc:
This is a sparse but comfortably built cabin. Several hunting tools adorn the
wooden walls and different kinds of bows are hung above the fireplace, where a
nice fire could be built. A metal box used for smoking meat is hanging above the
fire too, and often the smell of meat in the air. Some furs and pelts lie on a
desk in the middle of the room, and a few stools are placed around it. On a
shelf at the back of the room there is a partly finished longbow.
name: Inside a Hunter's Cabin

desc:
The river flows from the west to the east and the water is chilly and crystal
clear. The riverbed is rocky and several large rocks in the stream might be nice
hiding spots for fish.
name: River Farnen

desc:
You walk along a tricky path here, north of you a river flows through the
landscape and north of it a forest can be seen. Pebbles and small stones makes
it a little hard to keep walking without slipping a little now and then. The
path continues to the east.
name: A Tricky Path

desc:
You stand amidst different green and tall trees. The forest that surrounds you
is very lush and the sound of running water to the south might be the
explanation to this. A river is visible through the branches, it is rather calm
here and you cannot see any rapids. It is hard to see what lies in any other
direction but it seems like the forest thins to the north.
name: Lush Forest

desc:
The hills here are smooth and gently make bumps in the landscape. A forest is
growing to the south and west and you can see that it is a lush and healthy
forest. A few plants grow here but it is mainly grass that covers this hillside.
To the north much rockier hills are seen.
name: Southern End of Hills

desc:
You stand here on a hill, surrounded by other similar hills. All are littered
with sharp and jagged rocks, some as tall as a person. Many of the rocks are
covered with growth like small bushes and dwarfed trees try to grow alongside
the bushes. To the north you see even larger hills but south and east more and
just as rocky hills spread out.
name: Rocky Foothills

desc:
Large hills cover this spot, smaller hills are visible to the south but north
and east the same majestic sculptures of nature spread out. Many trees try to
grow on the hillsides and rocks large as wagons lie as thrown out by some giant
hand. Moss covers most of the rocks and trees making them look very old and
worn.
name: King Hills

desc:
The majestic hills spread out around you, smaller hills are visible to the north
and east. A few trees cling to the hillsides and boulders of different sizes lie
here and there. Several of them are covered with moss and the trees seem to be
old and a bit gnarled. A plume of white smoke can be seen far to the north,
while far east you see a forest.
name: King Hills

desc:
The hills grow larger to the west but to the east they slowly disappear. Looking
north you see a cabin standing next to a trail by the forest. Travelling seems
to get harder the further west you go. There are large hills to the south making
that way look somewhat tiresome too. The pungent smell of smoked meat is very
clear in the air.
name: Small Hills

desc:
In this high growing wild grass, many tall and thick spots can be seen, where
the grass has grown together in clumps. A couple of trees are spread out in the
grassland. A large forest grows to the north and west and a trail leads around
the forest to the west as well as to the south. Rocks, boulders and smaller
stones are strewn all over these grasslands.
name: Grasslands near the Forest

desc:
This is the border of a forest. The trees are randomly spread out, impairing
movement. Most of the trees are old and are now stretching high into the sky.
Bushes and other smaller plants grow in-between the scattered trees. A small
trail leads off to the west, where the forest also seems to be denser.
name: Scattered Chestnut Trees

desc:
The forest rim to the south follows this path east and west. Westward the forest
vanishes in the face of a plain, and north lies an increasingly steep hillside.
To the northeast the path, accompanied by an avenue of moist grass, climbs into
a forest-dominated area.
name: Track Bordering a Forest

desc:
You are on a green-gowned plain, ornamented with patches of golden flowers. A
path barely visible in the high grass comes from the west, while another one
enters the plain from the forest in the east. The two paths team up to make a
trail south to a strange formation of stone.
name: Joining of Paths

desc:
You are on the western edge of a fertile plain. To the west lies the domain of
eagles and goats, brash mountains with peaks covered by eternal snow. The
smaller shapes of hills spread out to the north. A small path here provides
passage into the hills, while east it will take you towards the forest on the
other side of the plain.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
You have entered a valley secluded by vertical cliffsides in all directions but
to the north. In the middle of this valley someone has created an eerie circular
stone formation. Seven crude arches of grey rock are facing you, each covered
with runes illuminated by a strange, internal light. Various scenes have been
carved into the stones.
name: Mystical Ring

desc:
The walls of black rock in this corridor seems to absorb every ray of light
coming from the outside. Every sound, however insignificant, echoes between the
sheer black stone all around you. The floor is even and easy to walk on, and
there seems to be a corridor leading south but it is impossible to tell what
lies there.
name: End of a Corridor

desc:
Two smudged wall mirrors have been mounted on the west and south wall of this
dark hallway. Broken glass lie scattered on the ground, although none of the
mirrors appear to be damaged. The rough stone floor is warm to the touch, and a
waft of hot air, reminiscent of a dry desert breeze, circulates the hallway. The
corridor turns north and east from here.
name: Dark Hallway

desc:
Blocks of quartz have been used as building stones for the walls of this
chamber. A red flame burns from a wall socket, its flickering light sending dark
shadows dancing across the blue-black wall surfaces. A narrow stairwell leads
down through a rectangular opening in the floor.
name: Crystal Chamber

desc:
You have entered an ancient place of worship or sacrifice. In the centre of this
clearing stands a huge stone bowl supported by four skull-shaped pedestals.
Broken skulls and bones litter the bowl, and from a small round hole a strange
black smoke emanates. A masked face is depicted on the rock- face to the south.
name: Silent Clearing

desc:
This place looks like an abomination of nature, a chaotic parade of misshapen
vegetation. Gnarled roots entangle your feet, stooping trees stretch bent limbs
towards the ground, and thick, foul-scented vines cover the cliff wall to the
south. Through a break in the trees to the west, you see something dark rising
towards the sky.
name: Impenetrable Forest

desc:
You are in really rough forest now. The soil underfoot is dry, and in effect
weary and bent trees appear to the south along with withered ash bushes,
obstructing any advance in that direction. Going back north now seems to be your
only alternative.
name: Impenetrable Forest

desc:
The conical shapes of tall, elegant firs are all around you, and nothing else.
It is not easy to keep directions, but you notice that the ground rises slightly
southwards. You can hear crows and croons from several birds, but you see no
signs of movement anywhere.
name: Tall Silver Firs

desc:
You move along a forest slope, on mossgrown ground littered with rocks covered
by lichen. The ground descends sharply to the north, and the forest seems
lighter in that direction. To the south, trees cling to a small ridge too erect
for safe ascent.
name: Tall Silver Firs

desc:
You are walking on the north side of a ridge in the forest. Near the top of it a
huge uprooted tree lies rotting among the pebbles and stones of a scree. To the
east, where the ridge fades to nothingness, the ground drops to a lower level,
as it also does northwards.
name: Tall Silver Firs

desc:
You are wandering in deep, dark forest. The moist forest floor has caused a
layer of humus to form around the base of the trees, nourishing mushrooms and
fungi. You can move in any direction, but from here every way seems just as dark
and uninviting.
name: Humid Forest

desc:
You are in a forest dominated by tall, evergreen firs. It is possible to force
your way west, deeper into the forest, but it might be hard to keep your bearing
in that omnipotent darkness. Through the the many trees to the east, you can see
the forest humbly making way for an old path.
name: Trailside Forest

desc:
You stand on sloping ground, the many firs preventing a good uphill view.
Looking north you get a fine view of a green vale in the forest, but the ridge
across it prevents scrutiny further north or west. From a particularly crowded
part of the forest, beneath a cliff to the west, you spy a spiralling band of
ascending smoke.
name: Sloping Forest

desc:
You are standing in a small vale on the south side of a overgrown ridge
continuing east and southwest. West of south the ridge joins a line of hills
lying south of the forest. The forested hillsides to the south seems to offer a
long but easy climb into those hills. You can also follow the vale east.
name: Forest Vale

desc:
This small forest vale lies between a ridge to the north and growing hills which
can be discerned some way to the south. A blanket of needles fallen from the
many firs covers the stubby grass. You can follow the vale east and west from
here, or ascend the fir-riddled slopes leading south.
name: Forest Vale

desc:
The watery moss saps your strength as you move through the forest. The almost
unfelt breeze sifting through the dense woods carries a faint reek of decay. To
the south and west the forest seems heavier but not so troubled by dampness as
here.
name: Humid Forest

desc:
You have waded into a sludgy mire. You sink knee-deep into the mud, making a
sucking noise at every advancing step. A serpentine strip of muddy water rimmed
by brown weeds crosses the mire, but it does not look deeper than would prevent
you from crossing it to enter the forest lying eastwards.
name: In the Mire

desc:
You are standing in spacious forest by the eastern edge of a quagmire. As you
study the darkening forest around you, it becomes obvious that the rough woods
are impenetrable in all directions but south.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
As you trudge along between the trees, a mound appears before you. In its side
you see a round hole about four feet high, too big to be the entrance to the
home of any ordinary forest animal. Peering into it you see nothing but complete
darkness.
name: Sparse Forest

desc:
You have slid into what can be best described as a mud bath. Water from an
underground stream must have mixed with sand and earth, and created this thick
yellow-brown liquid. The slippery granite floor which caused you to fall leads
up to the outside.
name: Muddy Pool within the Cavern

desc:
In this place the trees look to be in an ongoing battle for sunlight and space.
All around, intertwining roots and branches hamper your movement. You stumble
onward, trying to keep your bearings.
name: Dense Fir Forest

desc:
You move among prickly fir branches in the lower parts of a hillside, standing
about level with the ridge crest on the other side of the vale to the north. The
woodlands give way to the southwest, but still one or two fingers reach south
into the hills from the giant palm of the forest.
name: Sloping Forest

desc:
Perhaps climbing down from the hole above was not a very good idea. At the
moment, you are standing on a frail-looking platform suspended above a large and
deep pit. The area is illuminated by the red glow of the lava which fills the
bottom of the pit, several yards below. You can barely breathe, since the heat
and the billowing fumes of the lava make this a very uncomfortable place.
name: Platform over the Fiery Pit

desc:
That was your last mistake. You fall into the pit, and a few seconds later, when
you reach the lava, you quickly burn to death.
name: Fiery Pit

desc:
This is a small round antechamber, not more than ten feet across. It is dressed
in red velvet and the air is very dry. The floor is made of black stones placed
in circles. Above the centre stone a hatch leads upwards. A small doorway to the
south leads into a chamber where a sarcophagus can be seen along the opposite
wall.
name: Antechamber

desc:
You have entered a small chamber with walls of earth. Steel constructions keep
the force of nature at bay, making sure the ceiling doesn't crash down upon you.
Water is dripping off the walls and the smell of damp earth lingers in the air.
The stone floor is covered with gravel and a small stream of water flows out
through the stone door west of you since the floor is slightly sloping in that
direction. Along the eastern wall a statue of a dwarf stands in full metal
armour with a hideous mask covering his face.
name: Small Earthchamber

desc:
The corridor ends in this small chamber far below the ground. In the middle of
the room a pedestal has been placed, upon which a statue of stone stands. On the
eastern wall a huge dragonhead protrudes from the wall, its black eyes following
every move you make. The northern wall pictures dwarves fighting dragons and the
vaulted ceiling is painted black except where dragonfire illuminates the sky. A
corridor leads to the south.
name: A Small Chamber

desc:
The corridor turns to the east here. The earthy smell is now tainted with a
vague odour of stagnant water which seems to be concentrated to the middle of
the room. The walls here show huge terrible dragons flying across a pale blue
sky with a massive darkness looming behind. They almost look real.
name: A Dark Corridor

desc:
An earthy smell is strong throughout the humid air in this tunnel. Water is
dripping from the walls and algae are growing on the few stones that can be seen
in the damp earth floor or protruding from the walls. The tunnel makes a turn to
the north where it leads into a dark pool of water.
name: An Earth Tunnel

desc:
You have entered a burial chamber. A sarcophagus stands along the southern wall
covered with black velvet. On each side of the sarcophagus a dwarven statue
stands watching over it. The left one wielding a great axe and the right one a
large hammer. The walls in here are made of black stone, as is the floor. A
small arched doorway leads back north to the circular ante- chamber.
name: Burial Chamber

desc:
The walls and ceiling in this corridor are painted all in black, making the
darkness very immense. On each wall, east and west, a square crucifix made of
gold has been put. Every noise you make in here echoes between the walls a few
times before it fades out. South of you it seems like the corridor ends in a
large cave while north of you it enters a small chamber. The scratching of small
feet against the stone floor can occasionally be heard.
name: A Vaulted Corridor

desc:
This is the northwestern part of a large hall. The ceiling is painted in a dark
blue colour that fades into black over a vaulted exit to the north. There is a
vague earthy smell in the air that seems to be stronger to the east. A raised
dais stands along the western wall.
name: A Large Hall

desc:
This is the northeastern part of the large hall and the ceiling here is painted
in a dark blue colour. The walls are made of a black stone, delicately put
together. The earthy smell is quite strong here, through a hole in the north
wall where a stone has fallen out, a moistured earth wall can be seen.
name: A Large Hall

desc:
This is the southeastern part of the large hall. The ceiling fades into a light
blue colour in the corner, which makes a sharp contrast to the black stones in
the wall. The stone floor is cold to the touch and dust swirls as you move
around. Many statues of different dwarves have been put against the walls. Some
of them look like they could start moving any second.
name: A Large Hall

desc:
This is the southwestern part of a large hall. The wall is made of black stones,
delicately put together to form the picture of a dwarf on the south wall. The
ceiling is painted in a dark blue colour that fades into a lighter colour to the
east. There is dust in the corners and an faint earthy smell lingers in the air.
name: A Large Hall

desc:
This room is full of chests and other valuable things. Several racks have been
placed along the southern wall and the eastern wall pictures dwarves celebrating
the victory after a long war. West of you a large hall can be seen through a
stone door.
name: A Treasure Chamber

desc:
The air in here is dry and cold and there is a vague earthy smell. The dust in
the corners swirl as you pass by, but the floor is clean except from that.
Beautiful paintings covers the stonewall to the west, while the eastern wall
looks grey and naked. A handle can be seen protruding from the grey wall.
name: A Dark Corridor

desc:
A series of stairs built of stone lead up into the darkness. Dust has gathered
in the corners and rats are scurrying about. The walls here are built of the
same stone as the stairs and are very delicately painted with stories from the
past. The floor is cold and moisture can be seen seeping throughout the room. A
corridor leads deep into the darkness to the north.
name: Stairs of Stone

desc:
You have fallen into a dark and ominous pit and when you reach the bottom, the
trapdoor snaps shut behind you with a loud thud. The floor is covered by water
to your knees, dark and full of junk. Since it is impossible to see what you are
stepping on it is really hard to move around fast. There seems to be a well
built of stone in the middle of the room, it is only two feet across and has
hardly been used very often since green lichen covers it almost completely.
Walls of stone surround you in every direction, fifteen feet high, where they
reach the trapdoor above.
name: A Watery Pit

desc:
Despite the benefits of living close to the sea, some of the elves living near
here have joined together to grow a large vegetable garden. There are rows and
rows of finely groomed plants. They are growing corn, carrots, potatoes and many
other things here. Although the salt water from the Gulf probably makes it hard
to grow plants, the elves are doing a fairly good job.
name: Vegetable Garden

desc:
This village is filled with many pretty elven homes. Everyone in the streets
seems quite friendly. Numerous trees and small bushes have been planted to make
the area more beautiful. The village garden lies to the west and you see a
statue to the south.
name: Elven Village

desc:
You have basically reached the end of the shoreline. The water from the Gulf of
Lhun often runs into this area and it is quite wet. In most places you are
standing in several feet of water. Many reeds and other tall grasses grow in
here. Just a short distance off you can see the city wall. You can continue a
bit further to the north if you wish, but since this area isn't patrolled very
often you can't be sure its safe.
name: Swampy Shoreline

desc:
You have reached the end of the city. Here the swampy terrain runs directly up
to the city wall. Fortunately the wall seems stable since large piles of dirt
and sand help support it from the water rushing in and out. The area is filled
with tall reeds and grasses and a number of small bushes growing in the water.
You can also see some large piles of decaying wood. It seems likely that there
used to be houses in this area until the Gulf took over.
name: Marsh

desc:
The shoreline takes a sharp bend here and you find yourself in a small cove.
Towards the north the bank rises steeply and it appears that the elves divert
some water into a pond. You can see several small boats floating about on the
pond. The water there is calm unlike the water to the south in the Gulf. Many
trees and small bushes grow here just a bit away from the shore where the sand
has been overtaken by fertile ground. Off to the east the ground appears swampy.
name: Small Cove

desc:
Even though the shoreline bends north-east, the waters refused to be hemmed in
and seem all the more free. The beautiful sky above, with the gleaming sun,
paint a brilliant pattern upon the waves, and one can easily be entranced by
gazing into its clear depths. The pond could tell stories beyond number, about
countless ages and trials. An almost somber note can be seen far beneath the
surface, resting at the sandy bottom. Even the most beautific settings and merry
songs cannot fully erase the stain of the kinslayings ages before...and in the
mind of the elves in this fair city, it is something that should never be
forgotten.
name: Sparkling Pond

desc:
The waves lap briefly against the sandy beach to the north, then return to the
deep and clear water, further towards the centre of the pond. The surface
shimmers and sparkles, sending prisms of light in all directions and dazzling
the eye. If only the worries and problems of the world did not exist, and you
could float here forever, without care... The thoughts of things to be done are
quickly banished by your mind as these problems do cease to exist, at least for
a short while.
name: Sparkling Pond

desc:
The waves of the lake lap up against your craft, inviting you to come play and
dance with them and forget the cares of your mortal existence. 'Please,' they
laugh, 'don't return to the solid and boring earth, stay with us and relive the
best moments of your life and then experience even better ones.' The call of the
sea to the elves is no mystery and now it is obvious what entrances them so.
Occasionally an adventurous few drops leap up from the crests, and grace your
cheek and skin with their cooling presence.
name: Sparkling Pond

desc:
How lucky you are! Here, amongst the waves and ripples of this pond you are at
peace. Bobbing slightly, feeling the warm ocean breeze and the sun warming your
tired form, and listening to the soft water sounds, one cannot help but be
serene and thankful for this refuge. As quiet and private as these waters are,
they do not covet and hoard their kindness. They are more than willing to give
of themselves to any who would accept.
name: Sparkling Pond

desc:
You have passed the end of the quay and are now on a sandy beach. The water
quickly rushes in and out here and with each passing wave many small stones and
shells are tossed about. The shore continues to the east and you can go back to
the quay to the south.
name: Sandy Shore

desc:
This is obviously the jewelry store. The shop is brightly lit and the light
reflects off the many priceless gems displayed here. Although the gems and
jewelry are stored under a glass cover, the owner will let you look more closely
at a specific item if you wish. You can hardly imagine the value of all the
items for sale here.
name: Forlond's Fine Jewelry

desc:
The decor of this chamber tells you instantly that this is the mage guild.
Shelves here are stocked with numerous potions and rare herbs. These items are
mixed together in the many flasks and vials resting on the tables here in hopes
of perfecting some new spell. Numerous sheets of paper are lying around possibly
containing the secret ingredients necessary for some powerful magic. The room is
lit by several small dim torches and there are also several small iron braziers
probably used for heating liquids. You can go east back to Lhun Street or there
is also a stairway leading up.
name: Guild of the Arcane

desc:
This shop is filled with many racks filled with fine clothes. There is an
enormous selection here. You can almost certainly find something you would like
to wear.
name: Tailor's Shop

desc:
This looks like a store where you can find almost anything. The shelves are
stocked with all sorts of items. One side of the room has piles of torches and
lanterns. Another side has freshly baked bread and some tarts. You also see some
drinking containers.
name: Forlond's General Store

desc:
Although this shop is relatively small, it seems to have all your basic needs.
You can see shelves piled with leather armour and simple weapons like swords and
bows. This may be the only shop of its kind near here, since it is quite crowded
in here. You can exit to the east or west. A sign rests on a wooden counter in
front of you.
name: Gildor's Weapons and Armour

desc:
The smell of this place is enough to tell you that it is the fish market. All
around are fishmongers doing their best to sell their fish. Elves crowd around
trying to see who has the best product at the best price. Shops lie to the east
and west, Salmon Run Road heads north, and the fish quay lies to the south.
name: Fish Market

desc:
This road is accurately named. It is directly downwind of the fish market and
the fish quay and the smell of fish is quite strong. Although the smell might be
a little annoying to many people, the many elves on the street do not seem to
mind, and must be accustomed to it by now. The street continues north and a
number of buildings stand to the east and west.
name: Salmon Run Road

desc:
This road is accurately named. It is directly downwind of the fish market and
the fish quay and the smell of fish is quite strong. It seems unlikely, but you
almost think it smells worse here than at the market itself. Funny how the elves
strolling around here don't seem to mind. The street continues to the north, a
garden lies to the east, and a paved courtyard lies to the west.
name: Salmon Run Road

desc:
You have reached the end of Salmon Run Road. You only wish the fishy smell was
gone as well. This is a very crowded area. There are large buildings to the east
and north and many elves are running in and out. The road heads back to the
south and in the distance you can see some large fishing boats.
name: Salmon Run Road

desc:
This huge building contains the history of the elves in Middle-earth and
Valinor. If you wanted you could find an Elven account of almost every important
event in their history. In particular, you could read about the dreadful
kinslayings, the splendour of the secret city of Gondolin, the creation of the
world by Eru, or of the building of these fair havens. It is all here and you
are free to read whatever you like. Many of these texts are very old and you
should be careful not to ruin them. Salmon Run Road lies to the south and the
library's map room lies at the top of a spiral staircase.
name: Grand Library

desc:
This small room is filled with maps. To protect them from the passage of time,
they are all encased in glass. You can look at whatever you like but none can be
taken from this room. There are maps here from the old days before Beleriand was
destroyed and newer ones like maps of this town. There is a sign here attached
to the wall.
name: Map Room

desc:
After climbing the stairs you find yourself on a small stone platform on top of
the city wall. The area is about ten feet square. From here and from other
places around the city, archers could help defend the city. However in these
peaceful times, this platform is generally empty. Elves often climb up here
though since there is a fantastic unobstructed view of Ered Luin. The mountains
stretch off to the north and spread far east and west as well. Their snow-capped
peaks dominate the scene and in many places they rise above the clouds.
name: On Top of the City Wall

desc:
This is obviously the post office for Forlond. Letters are piled everywhere and
it is really quite a mess. Several elves are here working to sort all the mail.
It may take a while but you can probably find your mail or leave mail for
someone else if you wish. There is a sign resting on a table here. Just outside
the building, a circular stairway leads to the top of the city wall.
name: Post Office

desc:
In front of you lies the north gate of Forlond, the part of The Grey Havens
which lies on the northern shore of the Gulf of Lhun. The gate is high and made
of solid oak and banded with strips of iron. In several places intricate designs
have been made in thin lines of silver and mithril. Beyond the beautiful gate
you are able to see the high mountains of Ered Luin. To the south, Lhun Street
leads into the town.
name: Forlond's North Gate

desc:
This is one of the most crowded places in town. The road here is covered with
white marble blocks arranged in a square. At the centre is a large fountain.
Many elves are here chatting to each other and some of the elven children are
playing in the fountain. From the north you can hear some rowdy elves so most
likely it is a bar. To the east and west lie Salmon Run Road and Lhun Street.
name: Fountain Court

desc:
This area appears to be a meeting place for all the elves of the town. Since the
elves prefer the outdoors, they hold their town meetings outdoors as well. This
is basically a circular area bounded on the north by the city wall and the
imposing mountains of Ered Luin and on the south by Evergreen Park. Maple trees
and elm trees grow in abundance here and there are also several rows of pretty
flowers. Benches are arranged here in a circle for the townspeople so they can
discuss important issues. A board nailed to a tree here most likely contains
information relevant to everyone.
name: Outdoor Meeting Grounds

desc:
The elves love all types of trees and here they have planted rows and rows of
evergreens. You can see several varieties of pines and a number of spruce trees
as well. There are also some evergreen bushes here. Even in winter this park is
a beautiful green and the elves here often flock to it. The park continues to
the west and there are also scenic areas to the north and south.
name: Evergreen Park

desc:
This small street is bordered by many trees which keep it quite shady. Several
elves can be seen lounging around under the shade of a particularly large tree.
Some elven homes lie along the southern border of this lane and a park lies to
the north. Lhun Street lies back to the east.
name: Shaded Lane

desc:
This is really just an extension of the park further east. Many firs of all
varieties grow here. Even in the winter many elves come here to relax in the
shade. However there are also some fruit trees here. In particular, you see
several apple and pear trees. You may be able to have a snack while you relax
here.
name: Small Orchard

desc:
You have entered a quiet yard which is shaded from the sun by tall, broad-leafed
trees. This area has been paved with broad, smooth slabs of granite and the tall
brick walls which surround this enclosure stand almost fifteen feet tall. To the
east, a tall vine-covered gate stands between you and Forlond. To the west, an
impressive structure has been constructed amidst a forest of green trees.
name: A Shaded Forecourt

desc:
Numerous elven homes lie in this area. It is one of the more densely packed
areas of the town. Some of the homes are quite close together. It almost looks
like a separate community. Young elven children are playing in the streets and
they appear to be having quite a good time. A number of trees and bushes grow
along the streets and the elves have done a good job of keeping the area around
their homes beautiful.
name: Elven Homes

desc:
Should you wish to find a soft bed for the night, this is the place. Many
private rooms are here stretching off down the corridor. Each has its own
version of seaside decor and it is probably easy to find one of your liking.
Should you prefer a drink or some snacks before retiring for the night, the bar
is downstairs.
name: Fishnet Reception

desc:
Tucked away in the far shore of the pond, this beach is an idyllic setting to
relax in and dream. The white sand forms the dunes and ripples of the beach and
it feels both cool from the breeze and hot from the sun. Forming an elegant
skyline with the white sands is a sapphire sky, with clouds as white as snow and
as soft as down. The wind, having blown over the waves, delights you as it plays
through your hair and revitalizes even the most weary soul. Several homes lie in
the area built by elves who prefer a bit of isolation in this fair city.
name: Small Beach

desc:
Softly the sounds of waves breaking upon the coast brush upon the ear, filling
the soul with serenity. But beneath that the roar of the power and tumult of and
angry sea rumbles through the peace, reminding one of the wildness of the sea.
Both the unrestrained might and the graceful quiet of the sea are depicted in
this temple. On the walls are frescoes of gentle ripples that the sailors pray
for and love, while behind them, in a deeper blue, the stormy waves that sailors
fear rise up. In the centre of the room lies a sculpture of Osse and Uinen,
husband and wife, the two faces of the coastal waters.
name: Osse's Temple

desc:
Tucked away from the main thoroughfares criss-crossing the city, this garden
seems a quiet refuge from the otherwise hectic pace. No fishmongers disturb the
peace with their yells, no stench of fish spoils the breeze, and no raucous
clatter of wagons grate against your ear. Instead, the wind plays gently among
the elms, filling your mind with a soothing rustle, accompanied by the pleasant
murmur of the pond to your east. Its crystal waters seem a haven, beckoning your
tired body to come and join it in a delightful swim.
name: Elm Garden

desc:
This lane is quite peaceful since it is far away from the crowded streets in the
central part of town. The trees lining it make it quite shady and a number of
young elves are playing hide and seek with you as they periodically glance out
from behind the trees. The street continues east, a park lies north and a small
village lies to the south.
name: Shaded Lane

desc:
This room is reserved for private discussions among sorcerers. Here they can
share with each their knowledge of both old and new spells. Information about
how a spell works and when it probably should not be used can often be heard in
this chamber. The lighting is dim and it is furnished with several couches.
name: Magicians' Meeting Room

desc:
This softly lit room, illuminated with a pale blue radiance, serves as a
sanctuary for those that worship Osse or Uinen. Little in the way of sculpture
or artwork adorns these walls. Simple depictions of ocean scenes serve as pious
decorations. Contrasting images of the peace of a calm sea and the might of a
raging maelstrom come to mind as you rest here.
name: Acolytes' Sanctuary

desc:
Several guards are stationed here at all times. You are currently standing in
their living quarters. There are several bunks and a large table where you
assume they eat and probably play cards. You can only exit back to the west.
name: Small Barracks

desc:
Through a narrow fissure runs a route used by the dwarves in times of war.
Should the dwarven stronghold fall, this is where the last retreat would be
made. Little work has been put into the forging of the route, and judging by the
rough and natural walls full of long cracks and deep holes, the dwarven pickaxes
rarely pay this tunnel a visit. The fissure takes a sudden plunge upwards, but
cracks and holes provide a good foothold even for a heavy and clumsy dwarf to
proceed. A distinct draught of cold air indicates that an exit must be near.
name: Escape Route

desc:
A multitude of sharp rocks and cracked splinters lines the floor in the narrow
passageway under the mountain. Gleaming fragments of ore in the otherwise
pitch-black walls reveal the richness of the underground that lies beneath the
Blue Mountains. The cracked ceiling with its long razor-sharp protrusions nearly
touches the rubble floor in places, making the passage through the escape route
somewhat difficult. It would appear that the dwarves have not yet completed
construction in this tunnel.
name: Escape Route

desc:
In the depths of the mountain several cave systems take root, often extending
all the way to the open lands outside. This tunnel seems to be a part of one
such system, with a sudden breeze indicating that some form of exit may lie
nearby. Although the walls of this tunnel show some indications of widening to
allow easier passage, in general it appears to be one of those natural tunnels
which has been discovered during mining operations and further utilised because
it just happens to be heading in the right direction.
name: Escape Route

desc:
A smooth ceiling curving gently up towards the centre of the cavern makes an
impressive display of the astonishing skill of tunneling and carving that is so
commonly found among dwarves. Around the cavern the walls rise towards the
ceiling in a harmony of blue colours that seem to originate from the mountain
itself. To the north a heavy door is set, and a faint draught of fresh air comes
from that direction; yet there is no light to be seen.
name: Thrain's Escape

desc:
You entered one of the many small mines that the Dwarves are operating here in
the Blue Mountains. The tunnels around you are very small and twisty, as they
follow the poor ore veins of the Blue Mountains. The timbers look very strong
and safe: this mine is a good demonstration of the great construction skills of
the Dwarves. From here, you can climb a metal ladder on the wall of the main
shaft and exit the mine.
name: Dwarven Mine

desc:
You have reached the end of Lhun Street. There are far fewer people here than
further south on the street. Although you can see the north gate of Forlond
ahead of you, it appears that it is used very rarely. To the west you can see
some benches arranged in a circle. It appears to be a meeting place. Off to the
east is a small building.
name: Lhun Street

desc:
You are still on Lhun Street but you have moved away from the crowded part of
the street near all the shops. The pace is much slower here and you can enjoy a
leisurely walk. Up ahead you can see the north gate of Forlond and a park lies
to your west. On the east is a building with a greal deal of noise coming from
it, but there does not appear to be an entrance from this side.
name: Lhun Street

desc:
Certainly not as many elves bustle about this section of the street. You are
allowed the luxury of walking softly and breathing in the warm sea air and
gentle breeze. Those that are so inclined can stroll west along a beautiful
shaded lane under the shelter of many trees. To the north past the northern gate
lies Ered Luin, one of the few remaining dwarven strongholds. Several sailors
and other rougher looking characters saunter east to a pleasant court filled
with activity.
name: Lhun Street

desc:
You find yourself amid the busiest section of Lhun Street. At all times of the
day, elves and visitors to this fair city swarm about, carrying hapless folk
along in their wake. Perhaps its central location, between the armoury and
tailor's shop plays some part in this. The guards and adventurers that require
Gildor's fine merchandise spend much time here, and all elves need the quality
clothes offered by the tailor. Also, those hardy folk that will venture
northwards, to the Blue Mountains, must pass north to the gate. Southwards the
sailors and travellers flow, towards the ships and quays.
name: Lhun Street

desc:
This section of Lhun Street is bordered by many small shops. A building to the
west has many arcane symbols in relief around the doorway. It is hard to tell
that the street is formed from sandstone blocks since they are all slowly
wearing away. Many elves are roaming around here. Some appear to be wandering
aimlessly while other obviously have a specific destination in mind.
name: Lhun Street

desc:
This modestly furnished cottage is filled with a cosy, tranquil atmosphere. A
soothing, dim light cast by a few candles enlightens the room, revealing many
obscure and ancient artefacts that the landlord has brought along from his long
journeys. In the corner of the room, a fireplace and a comfy armchair are
located next to each other, allowing one to sit by the fire and perhaps to read
a book about gardening. Deciding by the choice of available books, it looks like
Shaodan really knows a thing or two about the legumes.
name: Shaodan's Garden Cottage

desc:
You are now in a small square where many nearby residents gather to relax.
Directly in the centre of the square is a large Elven statue. There is a small,
golden plaque mounted on the base of the statue. A small village lies to the
north.
name: An Old Statue

desc:
This is a small cottage made of pine and cedar, giving it a woodsy smell. Each
wall is lined with circular targets made for archery. Each target is crafted
from tightly bound straw, with a bullseye painted in the centre. The rest of
cottage is scantly furnished, giving the impression the owner spends little time
on anything but archery.
name: Kang's Cottage and Archery Range

desc:
This small dwelling leans up against the surrounding buildings, apparently
having little support of its own. It looks ramshackle and run down, giving the
impression its tenant is rarely in residence. A few broken items are scattered
around the floor. They seem of little worth, but may have once had some value.
name: Run-down Dwelling

desc:
This dimly lit room is full of fishermen and other townspeople having friendly
conversations over a few drinks. Fishing nets are hung from the ceiling and
contain items like stuffed fish. In several places, nets hold up the torches
which light this bar. It is a wonder this place hasn't burned down. You casually
listen to some of the conversations and hear a few extremely tall fish tales.
The stairs lead up.
name: The Fishnet Inn

desc:
Herbs of every variety seem to grow here, but they are hard to distinguish
admidst the mass of weeds sprouting up across the garden. Clearly no one has
tended to the plot in some time. A small gravel path connects the gate to the
east to a small wooden building to the west.
name: Overgrown Herb Garden

desc:
Tools of every variety are scattered around this workshop. Most lie strewn
across a great wooden bench that takes up half the room, but others are stacked
in corners, or just dropped in the middle of the floor. A small layer of sawdust
covers every available surface, include a thin cloth pallet that lies against
one wall.
name: Cugel's Workshop

desc:
A thick layer of shredded mulch covers the floor, and there are lush green
plants growing everywhere, many of them with brightly colored flowers. Above,
there is a steel and glass structure that encloses this beautiful garden,
helping to maintain warm temperatures inside even in the winter.
name: Lush Greenhouse

desc:
This fairly tidy room is very much like the rooms in a respectable inn, except
for the locked door and the barred window. The window does allow in a lot of
light and air, though, and provides a nice view of the Forlond lighthouse and
the Great Ocean.
name: The Jail
